method.cause: incident_stats grouped by custom_field:01KQC810AEHE7YEQ9MG04376QF ('Cause of Incident', multi-select — counts can sum above total) per week (created_after=Mon, created_before=Sun INCLUSIVE), mode standard+retrospective, status triage/active/post-incident/closed/paused, sample_ids kept as refs (cap 250/group). 'None' = cause not set (spikes on the in-progress week until triage catches up).
method.inform: Slack message search ('FT Incident for internal reference' + variants, after:window) filtered to channels starting 'ftcrm' ONLY (golive-* are not partner comms). Earliest mention per incident — multi-brand incidents count ONCE. delay_min vs incident.io reported_at (== incident channel creation ±1s). Author logged per entry (informer log).
method.mttr: Intercom conversations with team_assignee_id 50045975 bucketed by START week. START = statistics.first_assignment_at (arrival at SOC/SRE inbox) fallback created_at; CLOSE = statistics.last_close_at (standing convention; recover from conversation_parts when statistics null). P1 (tag 193658) = raw hours 24/7; non-P1 = office minutes Mon-Fri 09:00-17:00 UTC. Open conversations counted, excluded from medians. Never use sla_applied.sla_status.
method.p1_mttx: per-P1 named timestamps via incident_list include:timestamps,durations (private excluded, reported_at filtered to the week); kept for deep-dives, not currently on the slide. 'Accepted at' unstamped since ~5 Jun 2026.
method.api_trap: incident.io created_before is INCLUSIVE of the entire end date — always pass the Sunday, never the next Monday.
